articles.title: US still committed to talks with Iran, official says
articles.summary: US still committed to talks with Iran, official says A US official said on Thursday that Washington is still committed to finding a resolution with Iran and that technical talks are continuing amid renewed tensions between the countries, Reuters is reporting. This comes after reports of strikes hitting multiple areas in southern Iran, which officials are denying the US was involved in.

articles.title: Local official confirms strikes targeted naval site in Iran's Korarak
articles.summary: Local official confirms strikes targeted naval site in Iran's Korarak Jets targeted the naval military zone in the southern Iranian city of Konarak after two explosions were heard in the area, according to governor Mohammad Younes Haqqani, IRNA news agency reported on Thursday. Haqqani said that the naval area “was targeted by enemy fighter jets in two stages”, adding that rescue and security agencies were immediately deployed to the scene and an investigation into the attack is underway.

articles.summary: Netanyahu, Trump agree to continue coordination in phone call, Israel PM office says Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and US President Donald Trump reportedly held a phone call on Thursday in which they agreed to continue coordination between their countries on various fronts, according to a statement from Netanyahu’s office. It added that Trump updated the Israeli prime minister on “American moves in the Gulf”, following reports of renewed strikes on Iran, which the US has denied involvement in. Axios also reported that Trump spoke to Netanyahu on Thursday.

articles.summary: IRNA says Bushehr military site hit by US-Israel strike, denies reports of Bandar Abbas explosions Citing a Bushehr official, Iran’s IRNA news agency reported that a military site on the outskirts of Bushehr province was hit by a US-Israeli projectile, adding that the explosion sound in the city was caused by the air defence response. The government broadcasting agency also denied earlier indications of explosions in Bandar Abbas previously reported by Iran’s Mehr news agency.

articles.title: US reportedly not conducting strikes on Iran
articles.summary: US reportedly not conducting strikes on Iran CNN reported on Thursday that the US military was not responsible for carrying out the strikes in Iran in the past few hours. Al Jazeera also cited a US official confirming that the US is not involved in the latest strikes. However, there has so far been no statement from the Pentagon to confirm or deny their military involvement in the strikes.

articles.summary: Lebanese army to control 'pilot zone' in area occupied by Israeli forces, US official says The first so-called “pilot zone” in Lebanon, in which the Lebanese army will assume control of an area currently occupied by Israeli forces, will launch in a “matter of days”, with further zones being mapped out and planned, a US official said on Thursday. According to the official, Lebanon-Israel talks have moved to the implementation stage of the ceasefire framework.  This comes after Netanyahu said on Thursday that Israeli forces would stay in Lebanon for “as long as the need requires”.
